domains.title: Accessibility and interaction baseline
domains.summary: Keep contrast at WCAG thresholds, preserve visible focus states, label controls, support keyboard navigation, and provide loading/error feedback near the affected control.

domains.title: Responsive layout baseline
domains.summary: Design mobile first, avoid horizontal scroll, keep critical actions reachable, reserve space for media, and verify small phone, desktop, and wide layouts.

domains.title: Professional product interface
domains.summary: Use restrained hierarchy, dense but scannable layout, clear states, and semantic tokens. Avoid decorative card grids, one-note palettes, and generic gradient hero sections.

domains.title: Editorial or brand-led interface
domains.summary: Let the product, person, or place be visible in the first viewport. Use strong imagery or real media, not placeholder illustration, and keep the hero from becoming an isolated card.

domains.title: Accessible semantic color
domains.summary: Use semantic tokens for text, surfaces, borders, and states. Test contrast in both themes and avoid raw component-local hex values that bypass the design system.

domains.title: Readable type system
domains.summary: Use a coherent type scale, body text at readable sizes, line-height around 1.5 for paragraphs, and constrained measure for long-form text.

domains.title: Accessible chart defaults
domains.summary: Choose chart types by comparison task, provide legends and tooltips, avoid color-only encoding, and format numbers and dates using locale-aware rules.

domains.title: Operational SaaS and admin tools
domains.summary: Prioritize repeated-use efficiency, predictable navigation, table density, filters, saved views, bulk actions, and clear empty/error states.

domains.title: Landing page structure
domains.summary: Use a concrete first-viewport signal, a literal offer or name as the H1, one primary CTA, proof near the fold, and visible continuation into the next section.

domains.title: React and Next.js performance
domains.summary: Avoid unnecessary rerenders, keep server/client boundaries intentional, lazy-load heavy UI, reserve image space, and use Suspense or skeletons for meaningful loading states.

domains.title: Web app interaction
domains.summary: Use native controls where possible, label form fields, expose icon-only buttons with accessible names, and keep focus order aligned with visual order.

stacks.summary: Use semantic HTML, CSS tokens, responsive constraints, keyboard support, and browser-based verification across mobile and desktop viewports.
Keep component state local where possible, avoid prop drilling through layout shells, memoize only proven hot paths, and use stable keys.
Choose server components by default, keep client components focused on interaction, and avoid moving data fetching into effects without a reason.
Respect safe areas, platform gestures, dynamic type, 44/48pt touch targets, and native-feeling press feedback.
Use system controls, Dynamic Type, semantic colors, platform navigation, and accessibility labels/hints for custom controls.
